name: Palencia and the Castles of Tierra de Campos Loop
name_original: Palencia desde Becerril de Campos – circular por Castillo de Monzón de Campos y Castillo de Sarmiento
description: Easy 28.6-mile road cycling route in Palencia, passing historic Castillo de Monzón de Campos and Castillo de Sarmiento.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Becerril de Campos traverse the flat, agricultural plains of Tierra de Campos, characterized by open fields and historical landmarks. The region's terrain is generally gentle, with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for various cycling abilities. Road cyclists can expect routes that connect small villages and offer expansive views of the Castilian landscape. The area provides a network of roads ideal for long-distance rides and exploring the cultural heritage of the province of Palencia. Best road cycling routes around Becerril De Campos The most popular road cycling route is Palencia and the Castles of Tierra de Campos Loop, an easy 28.6 miles (46.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This route features historical castles and open plains. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puente Mayor de Palencia loop from Grijota, a difficult 64.9 miles (104.4 km) path. This route offers a challenging ride through the region's landscape with significant elevation gain. Local road cyclists also love the Sarmiento Castle – Castle of the Sarmiento loop from Fuentes de Valdepero, a 26.2 miles (42.1 km) trail leading through agricultural fields and connecting historical sites, often completed in about 1 hour 50 minutes. Road cycling around Becerril De Campos is defined by open plains, historical castles, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Becerril De Campos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Becerril De Campos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes around Becerril de Campos?
A: The road cycling routes around Becerril de Campos generally feature gentle terrain with minimal elevation changes, making them suitable for various cycling abilities. The region offers a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes, with 2 easy, 4 moderate, and 2 difficult routes available.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Becerril de Campos?
A: Yes, Becerril de Campos offers easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1160085690/palencia-and-the-castles-of-tierra-de-campos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia and the Castles of Tierra de Campos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 28.6 miles (46.0 km) long and traverses flat, agricultural plains, connecting small villages and historical landmarks.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Becerril de Campos?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with significant distance and elevation. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6953148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Palencia loop from Grijota&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 64.9 miles (104.4 km) path, providing a demanding ride through the landscape.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes in Becerril de Campos?
A: Road cycling routes in Becerril de Campos are defined by expansive views of the Castilian landscape, characterized by flat, agricultural plains of Tierra de Campos. You'll cycle through open fields, connecting small villages and encountering historical castles and cultural heritage sites.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by historical sites and points of interest. You might encounter landmarks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarmiento Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31469859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarmiento Castle – Castle of the Sarmiento loop from Fuentes de Valdepero&lt;/a&gt; specifically connects historical sites through agricultural fields.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Becerril de Campos?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1160085690/palencia-and-the-castles-of-tierra-de-campos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia and the Castles of Tierra de Campos Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40369863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Sarmiento – Puente Mayor de Palencia loop from Grijota&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical distance and duration for road cycling routes in this region?
A: The routes vary in length and duration. You can find shorter rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Palencia – Puente de Puentecillas loop from Grijota&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.5 miles (18.5 km), or longer, more challenging routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6953148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mayor de Palencia loop from Grijota&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 64.9 miles (104.4 km).

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Becerril de Campos?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the open plains, historical castles, and agricultural landscapes, as well as the network of roads that offer options for different ability levels.

Q: Is Becerril de Campos suitable for family-friendly road cycling?
A: Given the generally flat terrain and minimal elevation changes, many routes around Becerril de Campos can be suitable for families. Look for the 'easy' graded routes, which offer a gentle introduction to road cycling in the region.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: The region's open plains provide expansive views of the Castilian landscape throughout many routes. While specific named viewpoints are not highlighted, the vast agricultural fields and the connections between small villages offer picturesque scenery and opportunities to appreciate the rural environment.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Becerril de Campos?
A: There are 8 road cycling routes available around Becerril de Campos, catering to various skill levels from easy to difficult.
tips: Stone arch bridge over the Canal de Castilla, the Puente de San Juan is the entry point to Becerril de Campos from the west.

Masonry bridge erected in the 16th century and renovated at the end of the 19th century. The Puente Mayor crosses the Carrión River in Palencia, connecting the city center with the Allende El Río neighborhood. Originally made up of seven semicircular arches and prismatic cutwaters, it was later extended to allow road traffic. It currently has semi-cylindrical pillars and cutwaters with machicolation-shaped finishes, providing a fortified appearance.

The Cathedral of Palencia is dedicated to San Antolín, patron saint of the city of Palencia. Popularly known as 'the unknown beauty', it was declared a Historic-Artistic Monument in 1929. The current Palencia Cathedral, in Gothic style, stands on a previous Romanesque construction, of which only a few remains remain.

One of the best fortified castles in Spain. The Castillo de los Sarmiento has an extensive wall and keep. The castle, as seen today, was built in the 15th century, like many others, replacing an older castle dating back to the 10th century.
Cycling along the N-611 national road you will come across the castle of Monzón de Campos. A fortified hilltop castle, heritage structure from the 10th century.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as easy for road cycling, making it suitable for cyclists of all skill levels, including beginners. It features mostly paved surfaces and a relatively flat profile with gentle rolling sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows paved secondary roads, characterized by low traffic. You'll experience the 'endless plains' of the Castilian plain, also known as Tierra de Campos, with cultivated fields and rural landscapes.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is notable for connecting two significant historical castles. You will pass by the Monzón de Campos Castle, a fortified hilltop castle from the 10th century, and the Castle of the Sarmiento, a 15th-century fortification. Other highlights include Puente de San Juan, Puente Mayor de Palencia, and the Cathedral of San Antolín of Palencia.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Becerril de Campos. You should be able to find parking within the village to begin your ride. As it's a circular route, you'll return to your starting point.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Palencia region experiences a continental climate. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily uses public roads, which are generally accessible, it's designed for road cycling. Bringing a dog would typically require them to be in a trailer or basket. Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort, and respect local regulations regarding pets in villages and near historical sites.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths. However, if you plan to enter any of the castles or other attractions, they may have their own entrance fees.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through villages like Becerril de Campos and Monzón de Campos, you will likely find cafes, restaurants, or small shops where you can stop for refreshments or a meal. It's always a good idea to check opening hours, especially in smaller towns.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: For this easy road cycling route, we recommend bringing a well-maintained road bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump), and sun protection. Don't forget your camera for the scenic views and historical landmarks!

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route coincide with other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Canal de Castilla (Ramal de Medina), GR-89 BTT Canal de Castilla (Ramal de Medina), and Canal de Castilla (Ramal Norte-Sur), among others.

faqs.question: What makes this route particularly appealing for road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route stands out for its combination of easy accessibility for road cyclists and the opportunity to visit two significant historical castles, the Monzón de Campos Castle and the Castle of the Sarmiento. The low traffic on secondary roads provides a tranquil and culturally enriching experience through the authentic Palencia province.

waypoints.name: Cathedral of San Antolín of Palencia
